name: User Ops Swarm Evaluation Rubric
description: Scoring rubric for comparing single-agent baseline vs. swarm output

dimensions.name: business_insight_depth
dimensions.description: Does the output demonstrate genuine understanding of QSR user operations dynamics?

levels.1: Generic marketing advice that could apply to any industry
levels.2: Mentions QSR context but with surface-level understanding
levels.3: Identifies 1-2 QSR-specific dynamics (e.g., delivery commission, fulfillment constraints)
levels.4: Demonstrates deep QSR knowledge across multiple dimensions (channels, margin, operations)

dimensions.name: conflict_quality
dimensions.description: Does the Bull vs. Bear debate produce genuine organizational tension?

levels.1: Bull and Bear agree; no real tension
levels.2: Minor disagreements but both sides reach similar conclusions
levels.4: Sharp conflict across 3+ dimensions with specific, actionable objections

dimensions.name: risk_identification_completeness
dimensions.description: Does the output identify and assess risks across all five dimensions?

levels.2: Mentions risk in passing without specifics
levels.4: Identifies risks in 3-4 dimensions with severity ratings
levels.5: Comprehensive risk assessment across all 5 dimensions with mitigations

dimensions.name: execution_actionability
dimensions.description: Could the strategy be handed to a team and executed without further interpretation?

levels.1: Vague recommendations; no specifics on channels, timing, or owners
levels.2: General direction but missing key execution details
levels.3: Complete enough for a senior manager to fill gaps
levels.4: Detailed execution plan with timeline, channels, offers, and owners
levels.5: Ready for team handoff with success metrics and review checkpoints

dimensions.name: decision_clarity
dimensions.description: Does the Director make a clear, defensible decision with rationale?

levels.1: No explicit decision or decision contradicts evidence
levels.2: Decision stated but without clear rationale
levels.3: Decision with rationale that references key debate outcomes
levels.4: Decision with explicit conditions for success and review plan
levels.5: Decision with success metrics, risk controls, and named review checkpoints

dimensions.name: reflection_reusability
dimensions.description: Could the memory candidate actually inform a future decision?

levels.1: Generic or obvious lesson; no reuse value
levels.2: Lesson specific to this campaign but condition for reuse is vague
levels.3: Lesson with specific reuse condition but confidence score is absent
levels.5: Memory candidate with high confidence (≥ 0.7) and precise reuse trigger

note: If swarm total score is not at least 4 points higher than single-agent baseline, the swarm mechanism is not providing sufficient value.
